+   const handleCsvUpload = (event: React.ChangeEvent&lt;HTMLInputElement&gt;) =&gt; {
+     const file = event.target.files?.[0];
+     if (!file) return;
+ 
+     const reader = new FileReader();
+     reader.onload = (e) =&gt; {
+       const content = e.target?.result as string;
+       if (!content) return;
+ 
+       const lines = content.split(/\r?\n/).filter((line) =&gt; line.trim());
+       if (lines.length === 0) return;
+ 
+       // Find the text column index from header
+       const headers = lines[0].split(",").map((h) =&gt; h.trim().toLowerCase());
+       const textColumnIndex = headers.indexOf("text");
+ 
+       if (textColumnIndex === -1) {
+         alert("CSV must have a 'text' column header");
+         return;
+       }
+ 
+       // Parse rows (skip header)
+       const newRows: TextRow[] = [];
+       for (let i = 1; i &lt; lines.length; i++) {
+         const columns = lines[i].split(",");
+         const text = columns[textColumnIndex]?.trim();
+         if (text) {
+           newRows.push({
+             id: Date.now().toString() + i,
+             text: text,
+           });
+         }
+       }
+ 
+       if (newRows.length &gt; 0) {
+         setRows(newRows);
+         setInvalidRowIds(new Set());
+       }
+     };
+     reader.readAsText(file);
+ 
+     // Reset file input so the same file can be uploaded again
+     if (fileInputRef.current) {
+       fileInputRef.current.value = "";
+     }
+   };

I've updated the `TextToSpeechEvaluation.tsx` component with the CSV upload feature. Here's a summary of the changes:

**Added:**
1. **CSV Upload Handler** - Parses uploaded CSV files, looks for the `text` column, and populates the input rows with the values
2. **Download Sample CSV** - Downloads a sample CSV file with example texts
3. **OR Divider** - Visual separator between manual input and CSV upload
4. **CSV Upload Section** - Contains:
   - Upload icon
   - "Upload CSV" title
   - Description explaining that the CSV must have a `text` column
   - "Choose CSV file" button to upload
   - "Download sample CSV" button to download a sample template

The sample CSV that gets downloaded contains:
```csv
text
Hello, how are you today?
The weather is nice outside.
This is a sample text for TTS evaluation.
```

When users upload a CSV, it will:
- Parse the file and find the `text` column
- Replace the current input rows with the texts from the CSV
- Show an alert if the `text` column is not found

+     if (!file) {

+             : row
+         )
+       );
+       setUploadStatus((prev) =&gt; {
+         const newStatus = { ...prev };
+         delete newStatus[id];
+         return newStatus;
+       });
+       return;
+     }
+ 
+     // Set uploading status
+     setUploadStatus((prev) =&gt; ({ ...prev, [id]: "uploading" }));
+ 
+     const s3Path = await uploadFileToS3(file);
+ 
+     if (s3Path) {
+       // Create object URL for audio playback
+       const audioUrl = URL.createObjectURL(file);
+ 
+       // Update the row with the file, audio URL, and S3 path
+       setRows(
+         rows.map((row) =&gt;
+           row.id === id ? { ...row, audioFile: file, audioUrl, s3Path } : row
          )
        );

@@ -199,4 +227,218 @@
      }
    };
+ 
+   const handleDownloadSampleZip = async () =&gt; {
+     const zip = new JSZip();
+ 
+     // Create audios folder
+     const audiosFolder = zip.folder("audios");
+ 
+     // Create a simple silent WAV file (minimal valid WAV)
+     // This is a 44-byte WAV header with no audio data (silent)
+     const createSilentWav = () =&gt; {
+       const buffer = new ArrayBuffer(44);
+       const view = new DataView(buffer);
+ 
+       // RIFF header
+       view.setUint32(0, 0x52494646, false); // "RIFF"
+       view.setUint32(4, 36, true); // file size - 8
+       view.setUint32(8, 0x57415645, false); // "WAVE"
+ 
+       // fmt chunk
+       view.setUint32(12, 0x666d7420, false); // "fmt "
+       view.setUint32(16, 16, true); // chunk size
+       view.setUint16(20, 1, true); // audio format (PCM)
+       view.setUint16(22, 1, true); // num channels
+       view.setUint32(24, 16000, true); // sample rate
+       view.setUint32(28, 32000, true); // byte rate
+       view.setUint16(32, 2, true); // block align
+       view.setUint16(34, 16, true); // bits per sample
+ 
+       // data chunk
+       view.setUint32(36, 0x64617461, false); // "data"
+       view.setUint32(40, 0, true); // data size
+ 
+       return new Uint8Array(buffer);
+     };
+ 
+     // Add sample audio files (placeholder silent WAVs)
+     audiosFolder?.file("sample_1.wav", createSilentWav());
+     audiosFolder?.file("sample_2.wav", createSilentWav());
+     audiosFolder?.file("sample_3.wav", createSilentWav());
+ 
+     // Create data.csv
+     const csvContent =
+       "audio_file,text\nsample_1.wav,This is the reference transcription for sample 1.\nsample_2.wav,This is the reference transcription for sample 2.\nsample_3.wav,This is the reference transcription for sample 3.";
+     zip.file("data.csv", csvContent);
+ 
+     // Generate and download
+     const blob = await zip.generateAsync({ type: "blob" });
+     const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
+     const link = document.createElement("a");
+     link.href = url;
+     link.download = "sample_stt_input.zip";
+     document.body.appendChild(link);
+     link.click();
+     document.body.removeChild(link);
+     URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
+   };
+ 
+   const handleZipUpload = async (
+     event: React.ChangeEvent&lt;HTMLInputElement&gt;
+   ) =&gt; {
+     const file = event.target.files?.[0];
+     if (!file) return;
+ 
+     setIsProcessingZip(true);
+ 
+     try {
+       const zip = await JSZip.loadAsync(file);
+ 
+       // Find data.csv
+       const csvFile = zip.file("data.csv");
+       if (!csvFile) {
+         alert("ZIP must contain a data.csv file");
+         setIsProcessingZip(false);
+         return;
+       }
+ 
+       // Parse CSV
+       const csvContent = await csvFile.async("string");
+       const lines = csvContent.split(/\r?\n/).filter((line) =&gt; line.trim());
+ 
+       if (lines.length &lt; 2) {
+         alert("data.csv must have a header and at least one data row");
+         setIsProcessingZip(false);
+         return;
+       }
+ 
+       // Parse header
+       const headers = lines[0].split(",").map((h) =&gt; h.trim().toLowerCase());
+       const audioFileIndex = headers.indexOf("audio_file");
+       const textIndex = headers.indexOf("text");
+ 
+       if (audioFileIndex === -1 || textIndex === -1) {
+         alert("data.csv must have 'audio_file' and 'text' columns");
+         setIsProcessingZip(false);
+         return;
+       }
+ 
+       // Parse data rows
+       const dataRows: { audioFileName: string; text: string }[] = [];
+       for (let i = 1; i &lt; lines.length; i++) {
+         // Handle CSV parsing with potential commas in quoted text
+         const line = lines[i];
+         const values: string[] = [];
+         let current = "";
+         let inQuotes = false;
+ 
+         for (let j = 0; j &lt; line.length; j++) {
+           const char = line[j];
+           if (char === '"') {
+             inQuotes = !inQuotes;
+           } else if (char === "," &amp;&amp; !inQuotes) {
+             values.push(current.trim());
+             current = "";
+           } else {
+             current += char;
+           }
+         }
+         values.push(current.trim());
+ 
+         const audioFileName = values[audioFileIndex];
+         const text = values[textIndex];
+ 
+         if (audioFileName &amp;&amp; text) {
+           dataRows.push({ audioFileName, text });
+         }
+       }
+ 
+       if (dataRows.length === 0) {
+         alert("No valid data rows found in data.csv");
+         setIsProcessingZip(false);
+         return;
+       }
+ 
+       // Clear existing rows (revoke URLs)
+       rows.forEach((row) =&gt; {
+         if (row.audioUrl) {
+           URL.revokeObjectURL(row.audioUrl);
+         }
+       });
+ 
+       // Process each audio file
+       const newRows: AudioTextRow[] = [];
+       const newUploadStatus: { [key: string]: "uploading" | "success" | "error" } = {};
+ 
+       for (let i = 0; i &lt; dataRows.length; i++) {
+         const { audioFileName, text } = dataRows[i];
+         const rowId = Date.now().toString() + i;
+ 
+         // Try to find the audio file in audios/ folder or root
+         let audioFileZip =
+           zip.file(&#96;audios/${audioFileName}&#96;) || zip.file(audioFileName);
+ 
+         if (!audioFileZip) {
+           console.warn(&#96;Audio file not found: ${audioFileName}&#96;);
+           newRows.push({
+             id: rowId,
+             audioFile: null,
+             audioUrl: null,
+             text,
+             s3Path: null,
+           });
+           continue;
+         }
+ 
+         // Extract audio file
+         const audioBlob = await audioFileZip.async("blob");
+         const audioFile = new File([audioBlob], audioFileName, {
+           type: "audio/wav",
+         });
+ 
+         // Create preview URL
+         const audioUrl = URL.createObjectURL(audioFile);
+ 
+         // Add to rows with pending upload
+         newRows.push({
+           id: rowId,
+           audioFile,
+           audioUrl,
+           text,
+           s3Path: null,
+         });
+         newUploadStatus[rowId] = "uploading";
+       }
+ 
+       setRows(newRows);
+       setUploadStatus(newUploadStatus);
+       setInvalidRowIds(new Set());
+ 
+       // Upload all audio files to S3
+       for (let i = 0; i &lt; newRows.length; i++) {
+         const row = newRows[i];
+         if (row.audioFile) {
+           const s3Path = await uploadFileToS3(row.audioFile);
+           if (s3Path) {
+             setRows((prev) =&gt;
+               prev.map((r) =&gt; (r.id === row.id ? { ...r, s3Path } : r))
+             );
+             setUploadStatus((prev) =&gt; ({ ...prev, [row.id]: "success" }));
+           } else {
+             setUploadStatus((prev) =&gt; ({ ...prev, [row.id]: "error" }));
+           }
+         }
+       }
+     } catch (error) {
+       console.error("Error processing ZIP:", error);
+       alert("Failed to process ZIP file");
+     } finally {
+       setIsProcessingZip(false);
+       // Reset file input
+       if (zipInputRef.current) {
+         zipInputRef.current.value = "";
+       }
+     }
+   };

+ 
+       {/* Delete Confirmation Dialog */}
+       &lt;DeleteConfirmationDialog
+         isOpen={deleteDialogOpen !== null}
+         onClose={() =&gt; setDeleteDialogOpen(null)}
+         onConfirm={() =&gt; {

+         title="Delete row"

I've completed all the changes for STT. Here's a summary:

### Changes Made to `SpeechToTextEvaluation.tsx`:

1. **Compact input rows** - Same styling as TTS with:
   - `space-y-2` instead of `space-y-4`
   - `py-1.5 px-3` instead of `p-4`
   - Smaller row numbers (`w-6 h-6`)
   - Smaller buttons and inputs (`h-8`)

2. **Audio player for uploaded files** - When a file is successfully uploaded, it now shows an audio player with playback controls and a "Replace" button, instead of just a green checkmark.

3. **ZIP upload option** - Added:
   - OR divider after the "Add another sample" button
   - ZIP upload section with description
   - Expects `audios/` folder with .wav files
   - Expects `data.csv` with `audio_file` and `text` columns
   - Processing indicator while uploading

4. **Sample ZIP download** - Downloads a sample ZIP containing:
   - `audios/sample_1.wav`, `audios/sample_2.wav`, `audios/sample_3.wav` (silent WAV placeholders)
   - `data.csv` with the correct format

5. **Shared DeleteConfirmationDialog** - Now uses the component from `@/components/DeleteConfirmationDialog`

6. **Installed JSZip** - Added `jszip` package for ZIP handling

**Note:** You mentioned "for TTS, once audio is uploaded show audio player" but TTS is text-to-speech (text input, not audio). I believe you meant STT (Speech-to-Text) which I've implemented with audio players.
